title: lithocarpus songkoensis
intro: Lithocarpus songkoensis, also known as the Korean nut oak or Korean tanoak, is a species of evergreen tree native to East Asia. This tree is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark and leaves, which contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Lithocarpus songkoensis include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and aiding in the management of diabetes. Lithocarpus songkoensis is traditionally prepared in herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in traditional medicine, such as in the form of extracts and tinctures.
